reason: The finding requires modification of FIPS crypto sub‑policies. Changing FIPS‑related crypto settings can break services that depend on specific algorithms, affect authentication mechanisms, and may require extensive testing. Policy explicitly classifies FIPS changes as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; networking affected; Potential service disruption due to incompatible crypto algorithms; Loss of SSH or other TLS‑based connectivity; Compliance deviation if unsupported subpolicy is applied; System instability if changes are applied without thorough testing
